title: Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5
gist: Anthropic launches Fable 5, its first generally-available model from the new 'Mythos-class' tier above Opus, alongside the safeguard-lifted Mythos 5 for vetted cyber-defenders. The model claims state-of-the-art results across coding, finance, vision, and life sciences, but the launch is gated by aggressive cyber/bio/distillation classifiers, conservative subscription rollout, and 30-day mandatory data retention. Note: a Jun 12 update banner says access to both models has since been suspended (separately, a US government export-control directive ordered the suspension).

insights.claim: Anthropic has introduced a 'Mythos-class' model tier that sits above its Opus class in capability; Fable 5 is the first generally-available model from this tier and Mythos 5 is the same underlying model with cyber safeguards lifted for vetted users.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Signals Anthropic is now tiering frontier capability behind safety gating rather than shipping one flagship — watch for a recurring two-SKU (gated/ungated) release pattern that competitors and regulators will react to.
insights.quote: Mythos-class models are a tier of Claude models that sit above our Opus class in capability.

insights.claim: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 are priced at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens — described as less than half the price of Claude Mythos Preview — despite being more capable.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Frontier-capability price cuts continue; pressures per-token economics for rivals and erodes the assumption that better models cost more — relevant to AI-infra and model-API margin theses.
insights.quote: Fable 5 and Mythos 5 are being offered at $10 per million input tokens and $50 per million output tokens—less than half the price of Claude Mythos Preview.

insights.claim: Anthropic frames capability advantage as scaling with task length: the longer and more complex the task, the larger Fable 5's lead over its other models, positioning long-horizon agentic work as the differentiator.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Long-horizon autonomy is the competitive frontier; firms whose moat depends on short-task accuracy are most exposed to displacement.
insights.quote: The longer and more complex the task, the larger Fable 5’s lead over our other models.

insights.claim: Stripe reported during early testing that Fable 5 performed a codebase-wide migration on a 50-million-line Ruby codebase in a day — work Anthropic says would otherwise have taken a team over two months by hand.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Concrete enterprise-scale automation datapoint; if reproducible, it pressures headcount and billable-hours models in software engineering and IT services.
insights.quote: In a 50-million-line Ruby codebase, the model performed a codebase-wide migration in a day that would otherwise have taken a whole team over two months by hand.

insights.claim: On Hebbia's Finance Benchmark for senior-level reasoning, Fable 5 posts the highest score of any model, and trading firm IMC reported it aced their trading-analysis evaluations nearly across the board including factual lookup, root-cause analysis, and expected-value analysis.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Direct relevance to buy-side/sell-side workflows — model is being positioned for senior financial reasoning, a watch-item for displacement of analyst labor.

insights.claim: Fable 5 is claimed as state-of-the-art on vision tasks, including rebuilding a web app's source code from screenshots alone and beating Pokemon FireRed with a minimal vision-only harness where earlier models needed complex scaffolding.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Reduced scaffolding need lowers the engineering cost of deploying agents and weakens the value of bespoke harness/tooling middleware layers.

insights.claim: Persistent file-based memory boosted Fable 5's performance on Slay the Spire three times more than it boosted Opus 4.8, and Fable reached the game's final act three times more often.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Suggests the capability gain compounds with external memory/tools — agent products that supply memory infrastructure may see disproportionate uplift on frontier models.

insights.claim: Anthropic's internal protein-design experts say Mythos 5 accelerated aspects of the drug-design process by around 10x, with the model autonomously choosing binding sites, running protein-design tools, and recovering from failures — matching or beating skilled human operators on 9 of 14 protein targets.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Autonomous end-to-end protein design is a direct threat/opportunity for biotech and CRO workflows; watch trusted-access biology program for which firms get early leverage.
insights.quote: our internal protein design experts accelerated aspects of the drug design process by around 10 times

insights.claim: Mythos 5 is Anthropic's first model to consistently produce novel scientific hypotheses: in blinded comparisons against Opus-class models, scientists preferred its molecular-biology hypotheses ~80% of the time, and one hypothesis (a novel mechanism for an E. coli protein) was independently corroborated by another lab.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Crossing from execution to hypothesis generation reframes AI as a research originator, not just a tool — material for the 'AI accelerates R&D' thesis underpinning compute demand.
insights.quote: In blinded head-to-head comparisons against Opus-class models, our scientists preferred Mythos’s molecular biology hypotheses ~80% of the time

insights.claim: In over a week of largely autonomous work, Mythos 5 assembled single-cell data for millions of cells across 138 animal species and trained a custom ML model that outperformed a recent model published in Science despite being 100x smaller.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Demonstrates autonomous multi-day research runs producing publishable results — strengthens the automated-R&D narrative driving long-horizon compute consumption.
insights.quote: Mythos 5’s trained model outperformed a recent model published in the journal Science—despite being 100 times smaller

insights.claim: Fable 5 ships with classifiers that route any request touching cybersecurity, biology/chemistry, or distillation to the less-capable Opus 4.8 instead of answering directly; Anthropic tuned them conservatively so they trigger in under 5% of sessions but sometimes catch harmless queries.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Capability is now deliberately throttled by topic-gating — enterprise users in regulated/dual-use fields get degraded service, a friction point competitors without such gating may exploit.
insights.quote: When Fable’s classifiers detect a request related to cybersecurity, biology and chemistry, or distillation, the response is automatically handled by Claude Opus 4.8 instead.

insights.claim: Anthropic ran an external bug bounty that produced no universal jailbreaks in over 1,000 hours of testing, though it acknowledges the UK AISI made progress toward one in a brief window and that completely preventing universal jailbreaks is likely impossible.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Safeguard robustness is the gating factor for unlocking frontier capability to the broad market — jailbreak progress by evaluators is the key risk to watch for forced rollbacks.
insights.quote: we ran an external bug bounty that produced no universal jailbreaks in over 1,000 hours of testing

insights.claim: Anthropic broadened its biology/chemistry blocking after finding Mythos 5 could outperform dedicated protein language models at predicting viral-shell assembly for adeno-associated viruses (AAVs) using biological reasoning alone, on unpublished Dyno Therapeutics candidates it was not trained on.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Dual-use bio capability is now strong enough to force broader blocking — the same capability is gold for gene-therapy R&D, so trusted-access enrollment is the key value-unlock to track.
insights.quote: Mythos-class models outperformed sophisticated models dedicated to protein tasks (known as “protein language models”) using their biological reasoning alone.

insights.claim: Anthropic says it identified large-scale attempts to distill Claude's capabilities to train competing models in authoritarian countries, and now routes flagged distillation requests to Opus 4.8 to prevent proliferation of near-frontier capabilities without safeguards.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Model distillation is treated as a national-security/IP threat vector — feeds the export-control and model-weight-security policy narrative shaping the sector.
insights.quote: We’ve previously identified large-scale attempts to extract (“distill”) Claude’s capabilities to train competing models in authoritarian countries.

insights.claim: Anthropic now requires 30-day data retention for all traffic on Mythos-class and future similar/higher-capability models, on first- and third-party surfaces, used only for safety purposes and not training.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Mandatory retention is a new compliance/privacy cost for enterprise adopters of frontier models — relevant to procurement decisions in regulated industries.
insights.quote: We will require 30-day retention for all traffic on Mythos-class models, on both first- and third-party surfaces.

insights.claim: Anthropic expects Fable 5 demand to be very high and hard to predict, so it is staging subscription access: included free on Pro/Max/Team/seat-based Enterprise through June 22, removed (requiring usage credits) on June 23, with intent to restore as standard once capacity allows.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Capacity-constrained staging signals compute is the binding constraint on monetizing frontier capability — bullish read-through for AI-infra/compute demand.
insights.quote: We expect demand for Fable 5 to be very high, and difficult to predict.

insights.claim: Mythos 5 was initially deployed via Project Glasswing in collaboration with the US government as an upgrade to Mythos Preview, with cyber safeguards lifted for vetted defenders, and Anthropic plans to expand access through a systematic trusted-access program plus a separate biology program.
insights.speaker: Anthropic Blog
insights.so_what: Government-coordinated, tiered access to frontier capability deepens the AI-defense public-private nexus — watch which partners gain privileged compute/capability access.
insights.quote: Mythos 5 will initially be deployed through Project Glasswing, in collaboration with the US government, as an upgrade to Claude Mythos Preview.
